content: A thin cylindrical pressure vessel with closed-ends is subjected to internal pressure. The ratio of circumferential (hoop) stress to the longitudinal stress is

A cylindrical tank with closed ends is filled with compressed air at a pressure of $$500 kP$$a. The inner radius of the tank is $$2 m,$$ and it has wall thickness of $$10 mm.$$ The magnitude of maximum in-plane shear stress (in $$MP$$a) is ________
A gas is stored in a cylindrical tank of inner radius $$7$$ m and wall thickness $$50$$ mm. The gage pressure of the gas is $$2$$ MPa. The maximum shear stress (in MPa) in the wall is

A long thin walled cylindrical shell, closed at both the ends, is subjected to an internal pressure. The ratio of the hoop stress (circumferential stress) to longitudinal stress developed in the shell is

A thin walled spherical shell is subjected to an internal pressure. If the radius of the shell is increased by 1% and the thickness is reduced by 1%, with the internal pressure remaining the same, the percentage change in the circumferential (hoop) stress is

The figure shows a thin cylindrical pressure vessel constructed by welding plates together along a line that makes an angle $\alpha = 60^\circ$ with the horizontal. The closed vessel has a wall thickness of 10 mm and diameter of 2 m. When subjected to an internal pressure of 200 kPa, the magnitude of the normal stress acting on the weld is _________ MPa (rounded off to 1 decimal place).

The figure shows a thin-walled open-top cylindrical vessel of radius 𝑟 and wall thickness 𝑡. The vessel is held along the brim and contains a constant-density liquid to height ℎ from the base. Neglect atmospheric pressure, the weight of the vessel and bending stresses in the vessel walls.

Which one of the plots depicts qualitatively CORRECT dependence of the magnitudes of axial wall stress (σ1) and circumferential wall stress (σ2) on 𝑦?
explanation:
Given : σL = Longitudinal stress = σ1

It is due to the pressure at the bottom.

$ \sigma_1 = \sigma_L = C $

$ = \frac{P_{\text{bottom}} \times d}{4t} = \frac{\gamma hd}{4t} $

(Constant) σ2 = circumferential stress =

$ \sigma_2 = \frac{pd}{2t} $

Here, $P$ depends on $y$ and it varies linearly.

$ P = \gamma y $

At $ y = \frac{h}{2} $

$ \sigma_2 = \frac{\gamma hd}{4t} $

So, $ \sigma_1 = \sigma_2 $ at $ y = \frac{h}{2} $

content: A thin-walled cylindrical pressure vessel has mean wall thickness of t and nominal radius of r. The Poisson’s ratio of the wall material is 1/3. When it was subjected to some internal pressure, its nominal perimeter in the cylindrical portion increased by 0.1% and the corresponding wall thickness became t̅. The corresponding change in the wall thickness of the cylindrical portion, i.e. 100 × (t̅ − t)/t, is ________% (round off to 3 decimal places).
explanation:
Given data :

Poisson's ratio $(\mu)=\frac{1}{3}$

Hoop strain $\left(\varepsilon_H\right)=0.1 \%$

Hoop strain $\left(\varepsilon_H\right)$ is defined as :

$$ \varepsilon_H = \frac{\delta D}{D} = \frac{1}{E}\left(\sigma_H - \mu \sigma_L\right) = \frac{0.1}{100} $$

We know :

$$ \sigma_H = \frac{P D}{2 t}, \quad \sigma_L = \frac{\sigma_H}{2} = \frac{P D}{4 t} $$

Therefore,

$$ \varepsilon_H = \frac{1}{E}\left[\frac{P D}{2 t} - \mu \frac{P D}{4 t}\right] $$

$$ \frac{0.1}{100} = \frac{P D}{4 t E}(2 - \mu) $$

Radial strain $\left(\varepsilon_R\right)$ is defined as :

$$ \varepsilon_R = \frac{\delta t}{t} = \frac{1}{E}\left(\sigma_R - \mu\left(\sigma_H + \sigma_L\right)\right) $$

Since $\sigma_R = 0$ :

$$ \varepsilon_R = \frac{\delta t}{t} = \frac{1}{E}\left(0 - \mu\left(\frac{P D}{2 t} + \frac{P D}{4 t}\right)\right) = -\frac{\mu P D}{2 t E}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right) $$

$$ \frac{\delta t}{t} = -3 \mu \frac{P D}{4 t E} $$

From the previous equation :

$$ \frac{P D}{4 t E} = \frac{0.001}{2 - \mu} = \frac{0.001}{2 - \frac{1}{3}} $$

Substitute and simplify :

$$ \frac{\delta t}{t} = -3 \times \frac{1}{3} \times \left(\frac{0.001 \times 3}{6 - 1}\right) = -0.0006 $$

$$ \Rightarrow \frac{(\bar{t} - t)}{t} = \frac{\delta t}{t} = -0.06\% $$

Hence, the correct answer is -0.06%.

content: A thin gas cylinder with an internal radius of 100 mm is subject to an internal pressure of 10 MPa. The maximum permissible working stress is restricted to 100 MPa. The minimum cylinder wall thickness (in mm) for safe design must be ____________.
